original_hypothesis.title: SwapAndBridgeHandler Has No Upper Bound on Slippage Tolerance, Allowing Value Extraction via Malicious Swaps
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: setGeneralSlippageTolerance and setSlippageTolerance have no upper bound validation. The maintainer or emergency role can set slippage to 1e6 (100%) or even higher, effectively disabling slippage protection. With maximum slippage, any swap through the handler would accept 0 output tokens, allowing the swap route to extract the full input amount as MEV/profit.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: While genericSlippageTolerance defaults to 50,000 (5%), there's no cap enforced. Combined with a malicious or compromised swap route, this creates a value extraction vector through swapHoldings calls.

proof_of_signal.pattern: function setGeneralSlippageTolerance(uint256 _slippageTolerance) external onlyMaintainerOrEmergency { genericSlippageTolerance = _slippageTolerance; } — no upper bound check. If set to 1e6, minAmount becomes 0.

preconditions: Maintainer or emergency role sets slippage tolerance to extreme value
Swaps are executed through the handler with checkForSlippage = true

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: setGeneralSlippageTolerance(1000000) → all swaps accept 0 minimum output
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Requires maintainer/emergency role to set extreme tolerance
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: After tolerance is set until corrected

operational_questions: What is the maximum reasonable slippage tolerance for production operations?
Can pair-specific slippage override the general tolerance to mitigate?
Are there monitoring systems for slippage tolerance changes?
validation_requirements: Set slippage to 100% and execute a swap
Verify that 0 output tokens are accepted
Quantify value extractable through a single swap

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: minAmount calculates to 0 — swap route returns minimal tokens
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Value difference captured by swap route or external MEV

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: All swaps execute with no slippage protection

investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Slippage changes followed by large swaps

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
